steps.title: Read the vertex directly from vertex form
steps.content: The equation $y = -4.9(x - 9.1)^2 + 10{,}700$ is already in vertex form $y = a(x - h)^2 + k$. Read off $(h, k)$ immediately: the vertex is $(9.1,\ 10{,}700)$. No algebra needed.

steps.title: Interpret the vertex in context
steps.content: Since $a = -4.9 < 0$, the parabola opens downward, so the vertex is the maximum. The $x$-coordinate $9.1$ is the time in seconds; the $y$-coordinate $10{,}700$ is the height in meters. That means the plane reached a maximum height of $10{,}700$ meters at $9.1$ seconds. That matches choice B — B is correct.
